---
title: "Hospital Cardiovascular Cases in Barbados"
subtitle: "BNR CVD case-count briefing, 2023"
author: "Barbados National Registry"
date: "2026-04-28"
bibliography: []
citeproc: false
csl: apa
format:
  typst:
    output-file: bnr_cvd_cases_2023_v1.pdf
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    papersize: a4
    margin:
      top: 1.8cm
      bottom: 1.7cm
      left: 1.8cm
      right: 1.8cm
    mainfont: "Open Sans"
    fontsize: 10pt
    linkcolor: "#e87a00"
    fig-cap-location: bottom
---

# Hospital Cardiovascular Cases in Barbados

**BNR CVD case-count briefing, 2023**

Briefing created by the Barbados National Registry, The University of the West Indies.

## Key messages

- In 2023, BNR recorded 312 strokes in women and 312 strokes in men.
- BNR recorded 105 heart attacks in women and 141 heart attacks in men.
- Stroke cases remained above the recent five-year average through much of the year.
- A substantial share of cardiovascular events occurred before age 70.

{{< include ../../../../_shared/snippets/briefings/cvd/cvd_cases_2023_v1/why-this-matters.qmd >}}

{{< include ../../../../_shared/snippets/briefings/cvd/cvd_cases_2023_v1/what-we-did.qmd >}}

## Cumulative weekly cases

![Cumulative CVD cases in 2023 compared with the 2018–2022 average.](../../../../downloads/files/briefings/cvd_cases_2023_v1/figures/cases_2023_cum_week.png){width=100%}

## Cases by age, sex, and event type

![Age distribution of hospital-registered stroke and heart attack cases.](../../../../downloads/files/briefings/cvd_cases_2023_v1/figures/cases_2023_age70.png){width=100%}

## Interpretation

{{< include ../../../../_shared/snippets/briefings/cvd/cvd_cases_2023_v1/main-interpretation.qmd >}}

## Premature cardiovascular disease

{{< include ../../../../_shared/snippets/briefings/cvd/cvd_cases_2023_v1/premature-disease.qmd >}}

## Outputs

Tables, figure data, metadata, and build records are available in the online briefing.

## Citation

Barbados National Registry. *Hospital Cardiovascular Cases in Barbados: BNR CVD case-count briefing, 2023*. Barbados National Chronic Disease Registry, The University of the West Indies.
